title: Diasperus rigens

The classification of Diasperus rigens places it within the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Further classification also includes the subclass Rosidae, a clade of flowering plants that are thought to have evolved from a common ancestor. The family Euphorbiaceae, to which Diasperus rigens belongs, is a group of plants that are often characterized by their milky sap and lobed or palmate leaves. Overall, the classification of Diasperus rigens reflects its evolutionary relationships to other flowering plants within the Rosidae clade.
